quick_facts.label: Jurisdiction
quick_facts.value: This is not one uniform all-India recruitment examination: government ITIs are administered day to day by State Governments or Union Territory Administrations, which issue their own recruitment rules and notifications.

quick_facts.label: Role targeted
quick_facts.value: The process is used to select trade-specific vocational instructors for government ITIs—for example, Electrician, Fitter, COPA/ICTSM, Mechanic Motor Vehicle, or Sewing Technology—rather than a general teaching post.

quick_facts.label: Core qualification pattern
quick_facts.value: Eligibility is normally tied to the notified trade. Official rules can accept relevant engineering degrees, diplomas/advanced diplomas, or NTC/NAC/STC trade credentials, usually paired with specified relevant experience; exact alternatives differ by State/UT and trade.

quick_facts.label: Instructor-training credential
quick_facts.value: CITS/NCIC is an important instructor-training credential in the ITI system. DGT’s CITS programme trains candidates in trade skills and training methodology, while recruitment notifications determine whether NCIC/CITS is essential, preferred, or required after appointment.

quick_facts.label: Selection method
quick_facts.value: There is no guaranteed common paper pattern. DGT model guidance contemplates a State-level teacher-eligibility test for skills, competencies and aptitude, while individual rules can prescribe other elements such as practical tests, document verification, merit lists, or interviews.

sections.heading: What this recruitment examination is for
paragraphs: “State ITI Vocational Instructor Recruitment Examination” is a useful umbrella name for State- or UT-run selection processes that fill vocational-instructor posts in government Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs). It is not a centrally administered national examination with one permanent syllabus or calendar: DGT sets national policy, standards, instructor training and trade-testing functions, but State Governments and UT Administrations control day-to-day ITI administration.

The selected person teaches and supervises training in a specific vocational trade. Official recruitment rules illustrate the trade-specific nature of the post by listing separate instructor positions and qualifications for areas such as Electrician, Mechanic Motor Vehicle, Electronics Mechanic, ICT System Maintenance, Carpentry, Plumber, COPA and Sewing Technology.
bullets: Treat the State/UT recruitment notification—not coaching material or a generic exam page—as the controlling document for post name, trade, vacancies, reservation, age rules, eligibility, selection stages and appointment conditions.
Do not confuse this recruitment process with CITS admission. CITS is instructor preparation/certification; a State ITI recruitment process is for public employment.

sections.heading: Eligibility: assess it trade by trade
paragraphs: Applicants should match their qualification and experience to the exact trade named in the advertisement. A representative official rule for vocational instructors permits alternative routes: a relevant B.Voc/engineering degree with one year of relevant experience, a relevant three-year diploma or DGT advanced diploma with two years of experience, or an NTC/NAC/STC in the relevant trade with three years of relevant experience. The acceptable disciplines and any additional requirements vary by trade.

CITS leading to the National Craft Instructor Certificate (NCIC) is central to the vocational-instructor ecosystem. DGT describes CITS as training in both technical skills and training methodology; its 2026–27 admission notice identifies NTC, NAC, diploma and degree holders as eligible entrants. However, an applicant must follow the recruitment notice for whether NCIC/CITS is mandatory at application, merely desirable, or to be acquired under service rules.
bullets: Verify the exact trade mapping—for example, Electrical qualifications for Electrician versus Automobile/Mechanical qualifications for Mechanic Motor Vehicle.
Count only experience that the notification recognizes, and preserve employer certificates showing dates, trade/role and nature of duties.

Check additional trade-specific conditions. Official rules may require credentials such as a valid LMV driving licence for a Mechanic Motor Vehicle instructor or shorthand/typing standards for Stenographer & Secretarial Assistant.
Check domicile, age, category/reservation, language, medical fitness and document-format requirements only from the current State/UT notice.

sections.heading: Selection format and assessment
paragraphs: The format is notification-dependent. DGT’s model career-progression guidance proposes a State-level teacher-eligibility test, conducted by the State recruiting agency, to assess prospective ITI trainers’ skills, competencies and aptitude; the model states that such a score would be valid for two years. This is guidance for States, not proof that every current recruitment uses an identical test.

Selection may also be practical or trade-demonstration based. For example, Ladakh recruitment rules provide for selection by a practical test for a promotion channel to vocational instructor. Therefore candidates should not assume that a written test alone decides recruitment, or that a practical test, interview, or teaching demonstration will necessarily appear in every State.
bullets: Read the notification and syllabus for the weighting and qualifying rules for written, practical, teaching-skill, interview and document-verification stages.
If a written examination is prescribed, prepare both the notified trade domain and any stated pedagogy, aptitude, language, general-awareness or computer components.
If a practical/skill test is prescribed, expect assessment against the actual tools, safety practices, fault-finding, procedures and outputs relevant to the notified trade.

sections.heading: How to apply safely
paragraphs: Apply only through the official portal or instructions named in the relevant State/UT recruiting-agency or skill-development/technical-education department notification. Since ITIs are administered by States and UTs, application dates, fees, correction windows, documents and the mode of application can change with each recruitment cycle.

Before submitting, create a trade-wise eligibility file: educational certificates and marksheets, NTC/NAC/STC or diploma/degree evidence, NCIC/CITS certificate if held or required, experience certificates, identity and category certificates, photographs/signature, and any trade-specific licence. Official recruitment advertisements commonly require candidates to upload proof of the advertised qualifications and experience.
bullets: Download and save the advertisement, instructions, submitted form, fee receipt and final confirmation page.
Use the post and trade code exactly as published; applying under a similar-sounding trade can make a candidate ineligible.
Recheck the cut-off date for age, qualification and experience before final submission.
Watch the official notice board for admit cards, corrigenda, answer keys, skill-test notices, document-verification lists and final results.

sections.heading: Preparation strategy
paragraphs: Start with the current notification’s syllabus and selection scheme, then build preparation around the exact trade. For vocational-instructor work, high-value preparation combines trade theory, hands-on procedures, workshop safety, tools and measurement, troubleshooting, quality practices and the ability to explain and demonstrate a task to trainees.

Add teaching-method preparation where the selection rules or job requirements call for it. DGT describes CITS as combining trade technology, engineering/vocational calculation and science, and training methodology/teaching practice. These areas provide a sound framework for developing the instructional capability expected of an ITI instructor, even though they do not replace the notified recruitment syllabus.
bullets: Revise the current curriculum and occupational standards for the relevant ITI trade, not a generic “ITI instructor” question bank.
Practise teaching one workshop task: state the objective, demonstrate safely, let a trainee perform it, observe errors, assess the output and give feedback.
Prepare concise explanations of safety, PPE, workshop discipline, tool selection, measurement and common trade faults.
Maintain originals and clear scans of all credentials early; document scrutiny can be decisive even after an examination or skill test.

sections.heading: What selection can lead to
paragraphs: A successful candidate may be appointed as a trade-specific vocational instructor in a government ITI, subject to the vacancy, merit/selection list, verification and appointment conditions in the State/UT process. The role supports practical vocational training and the transfer of hands-on skills to ITI trainees.

Terms after selection remain jurisdiction-specific. For example, official Ladakh rules show distinct service conditions and a CITS/NCIC-linked progression provision for its vocational-instructor cadre; this should not be treated as a salary or progression promise for other States or UTs. Always rely on the applicable recruitment rules and appointment order.
bullets: Appointment is not confirmed merely by clearing one stage; candidates may still need to pass document verification, medical/character checks and other stated conditions.
CITS/NCIC can strengthen long-term instructor readiness and may be a mandatory qualification or service requirement depending on the applicable rules.
